FR-001.title: Applying a set of requirement changes twice leaves the living spec byte-for-byte unchanged

FR-002.title: Idempotency holds for every ordered same-heading verb pair, distinct bodies per verb

FR-003.title: Idempotency holds for every ordered same-heading verb triple, distinct bodies per verb

FR-004.title: Repeated add-and-rename folds never grow the requirement heading count

FR-005.title: Add plus rename of the same heading yields one requirement under the renamed heading

FR-006.title: Add plus edit of the same heading yields one requirement carrying the edited body

FR-007.title: Chained renames resolve to a final heading and rename cycles terminate

FR-008.title: Fold behavior for non-overlapping headings is unchanged

FR-009.title: The writer acts on every recognised flag in a single invocation

FR-010.title: The writer reports each kind of data recorded in a multi-flag invocation

FR-011.title: Delta parsing and its grammar live in their own module with no file access

FR-012.title: The living-spec fold-back step and its helpers live in their own module

FR-013.title: Task syncing, marker parsing, and checkbox writing live in their own module

FR-014.title: Capture handling lives in its own module

FR-015.title: The original script keeps the CLI, lifecycle, atomic write, migration, journal, and guards

FR-016.title: The command line interface does not change — same flags, output, and exit codes

FR-017.title: No command body, hook, preset, or calling script requires editing

FR-018.title: Every new module is included in the runtime packing list

FR-019.title: Shape-parity, command-emissions, and packaging checks all pass

FR-020.title: The spec-kit extension changelog carries a user-facing entry for each half

FR-021.title: Reference docs describing the script's shape are updated in the same change

context: constraint: CLI must not change — no caller, hook, or command body may need editing
constraint: package-manifest.py --check must pass so the release archive stays complete
constraint: #465 lands and goes green before the #458 split, as two separate commits
intent: Make living-spec folding repeatable, then split the oversized context-writing script into focused modules without changing its command line
expectations: No change to the command line interface — no new or renamed flags
No version bump anywhere, including extension.yml
No changes to the root README or root CHANGELOG
No TypeScript-side refactor, even though similar growth was noted there
No CI file-size guardrail — noted in the issue as a follow-up, not built here
approach: Make the add verb resolve its heading through the delta set's rename map before checking existence and before appending, and let a same-heading edit supply the final body — that makes every verb combination repeatable. Then move the code into five focused sibling modules with a one-way dependency order, re-exporting every moved name from the original file so no caller changes. Split the flag dispatch so additive capture flags all run while lifecycle modes stay exclusive.

decisions.decision: Fix the fold by resolving ADDED through the rename map rather than post-normalizing the document
decisions.why: The break is precisely that ADDED checks existence against pre-rename state; asking the post-rename question fixes both broken pairs locally and leaves non-overlapping deltas untouched
decisions.rejected: A whole-document deduplication pass after all verbs apply — it hides the duplication instead of stopping it and has to guess a winner with less information about intent

decisions.decision: Create five new modules, not the four the issue names — spec_context.py holds the shared store
decisions.why: The four named modules all need the context read/write primitives, so leaving them in write-context.py forces an import cycle that Python does not survive for a hyphenated module; a bottom shared module makes the graph a clean one-way fan
decisions.rejected: Injecting the primitives as function arguments, and lazy imports inside function bodies — the latter hides a dependency from the packaging gate, which derives what ships by scanning imports

decisions.why: Four external importers call its internals directly; the requirement that no caller needs editing is only satisfiable if the old names still resolve, and it makes the move provably behavior-neutral
decisions.rejected: Repointing every importer at the new modules — breaks the no-caller-edits constraint and spreads the blast radius into the eval skills

decisions.decision: Capture flags become additive; lifecycle modes keep the first-match-wins ladder
decisions.why: The reported data loss is entirely within the composable capture group; lifecycle modes are alternative readings of one invocation and running several would be an unrequested behavior change to a command line that must not change
decisions.rejected: Making everything additive, and making the flag conflict an error — the writes are best-effort by design and must never fail the host command

decisions.decision: Prove the command line is unchanged by differential comparison against the pre-split script
decisions.why: A reviewer cannot verify an unchanged interface by reading a diff that moves a thousand lines; running the flag matrix against both versions turns the claim into evidence
decisions.rejected: Relying on the existing suite, which covers the behavior it happens to exercise rather than the full flag surface

decisions.decision: A rename chain that loops back on itself is dropped rather than applied
decisions.why: A cycle names no final heading, so it is unsatisfiable input; skipping it matches how unmatched targets already behave and is the only choice that stays idempotent
decisions.rejected: Stopping the walk at the revisited heading and applying the partial chain — that renamed the section on the second apply and grew the document

plan.summary: Rename-aware add fixes every broken verb combination; the split needs a fifth shared-store module and full re-export to keep the CLI and every caller untouched
plan.key_finding: Four things outside the script import it as a module and call its internals directly — derive-from-files, status-context, both Python test suites, and the living-spec eval check — so re-export is the load-bearing compatibility mechanism, not a nicety

tasks.summary: 30 tasks across 6 phases — setup harnesses, red-first proofs, the fold fix (commit one), the dispatch fix and the five-module split (commit two), then docs and the full gate run

implement.summary: Fold repeatability fixed for every verb combination, the dropped-capture-flag bug fixed, and the 1860-line writer split into five focused modules with the command line proven byte-identical
implement.key_finding: The re-export surface is the load-bearing compatibility mechanism — four things outside the script import it as a module and call its internals, so all 74 names had to keep resolving for the no-caller-edits constraint to hold

T001.did: Built the CLI differential harness — 34 invocations covering every dispatch path, comparing exit code, stdout, stderr, and the resulting context file with wall-clock stamps scrubbed

T003.did: Recorded the pre-change baseline and verified it round-trips against both a stored result file and a staged copy of the scripts directory

T002.did: Built the idempotency matrix helper — generates a delta spec for any verb combination against one shared heading with a distinct body per verb, which is what makes the add-plus-edit break visible

T004.did: Wrote the table-driven test over all 12 ordered same-heading verb pairs; verified red for exactly the 4 known-broken pairs

T005.did: Wrote the unbounded-growth test — 5 applies of an add-and-rename set, asserting the heading count stays at 1; verified red at 5 headings

T006.did: Wrote the table-driven test over all 24 ordered same-heading verb triples plus rename-chain, rename-cycle, and pre-corrupted-document cases; verified red

T007.did: Wrote the multi-flag dispatch tests — two capture flags, all capture flags, classification alongside a decision, and the exclusivity of lifecycle modes; verified red on the dropped flags

T008.did: Added the rename-map builder and chain resolver; a rename chain that loops back on itself is dropped as unsatisfiable rather than applied

T009.did: Made the add verb resolve its heading through the rename map before both the existence check and the append, and retitle the section to the resolved heading

T010.did: Let a same-heading edit body win over the added body so an add-plus-edit pair settles on one body instead of alternating

T011.did: Ran the full matrix: all 12 pairs, all 24 triples, all 4 singles and all 12 disjoint pairs idempotent (was 21 of 36 broken combinations, now 0); growth flat at 1 heading across 5 applies

T012.did: Confirmed the living-spec eval's repeatability assertion now passes for an add-plus-edit fold, and fails on the same input against the pre-fix code

T013.did: Drift-proofed: reverted the fix and the matrix went red at 21 failures with the eval assertion failing, restored it and both went green

T014.did: Split the dispatch into an additive capture group that all runs and an exclusive lifecycle ladder, with the default step update still suppressed whenever any capture flag is present; classification is validated before anything is written so a bad value records nothing

T015.did: Moved reporting inline with each capture action so one line prints per action taken and a single-flag call still prints exactly one

T016.did: Ran the differential harness against the pre-change baseline: all 34 invocations identical in exit code, stdout, stderr, and resulting context file

T017.did: Created the shared store module — context read and atomic write, feature-directory resolution, git helpers, history log accessors and predicates, and the canonical step and status vocabulary

T018.did: Moved delta parsing and its grammar into its own module; no file access, so it can be exercised on a string

T019.did: Moved the additive capture writers — field setting, living-spec names, decisions, verifications, concerns, expectations, context, coverage, step summaries, classification, and entry coercion

T020.did: Moved task-marker parsing, checkbox writing, the per-task journal and append log, materialization, and the tasks.md backstop sync

T021.did: Moved the fold — requirement spans, the rename map and resolver, the delta applier, the initial scaffold, target resolution, and the fold entry point

T022.did: Reduced the original script from 1926 to 618 lines, keeping the command line, the step lifecycle, journal finish and advance, terminal promotion, and the no-regress guard, and re-exporting every moved name

T023.did: Added the five new modules to the runtime packing list; the gate now reports 14 runtime scripts with the closure matching in both directions

T024.did: Ran the packaging gate, shape parity, and both command-assembly checks — all clean; the ticket also named check-command-emissions.py, which does not exist in this repo

T025.did: Ran the differential harness against the pre-change baseline after the split: all 34 invocations identical

T026.did: Confirmed all 74 names the pre-split module exposed still resolve through it, and that status-context.py and derive-from-files.py run unmodified

T027.did: Added user-facing unreleased changelog entries for the fold fix, the dropped-flag fix, and the split

T028.did: Documented the new module layout, the dependency order, the re-export contract, the packing-list rule, the additive-vs-exclusive dispatch, and the fold repeatability rule

T029.did: Described the capture scripts' one-way dependency order and the packing-list rule in the architecture overview

T030.did: Ran the full verification set: 355 Python tests, 1599 jest tests across 118 suites, tsc compile, packaging gate, shape parity, both command-assembly checks, and the 34-invocation CLI parity comparison — all green

verified.what: Fold idempotency matrix — all verb combinations
verified.result: green; 4 singles, 12 ordered pairs, 24 ordered triples, 12 disjoint pairs, rename chains and cycles, and a pre-corrupted document all idempotent (36 of 36, was 21)

verified.what: Drift proof — the new tests fail against the pre-fix code
verified.command: git checkout HEAD -- speckit-extension/scripts/write-context.py && python3 -m unittest speckit-extension.tests.test_living_specs -k FoldIdempotencyMatrix
verified.result: 21 failures reverted, 0 restored; the eval s idempotent assertion also fails on its own fold output pre-fix

verified.what: Living-spec eval repeatability assertion on an add-plus-edit fold
verified.result: 6 pass / 0 fail (was 5 pass / 1 fail on the same input)

verified.what: Command line unchanged across the split
verified.result: 34 of 34 invocations identical in exit code, stdout, stderr, and resulting context file

verified.what: Every name the pre-split module exposed still resolves through it
verified.command: dir() comparison against a staged pre-change copy
verified.result: 74 of 74 names resolvable; no caller edited
tsc clean; 1599 tests across 118 suites pass
OK — 14 runtime scripts, closure matches the declared list in both directions

verified.what: The split works from a real install path
verified.command: specify extension add ./speckit-extension --dev --force; then a --decision --verified call against the installed copy
verified.result: all five new modules installed; both flags recorded and both reported

concerns.note: The ticket named check-command-emissions.py as a gate to run; no such script exists in this repo. The command-body gates that do exist (check-shape-parity.py, assemble-nodes.py --check, build-commands.py --check) were run instead and the spec artifacts were corrected to name them.

The split needed five new modules, not the four the issue names — a shared store module (spec_context.py) is what keeps the dependency graph acyclic, since the fold and the capture writers both need the context read/write primitives.

last_action: all 30 tasks done — 355 python + 1599 jest tests pass, 34/34 CLI invocations identical, all gates clean
